LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JOHN WICKER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH COLLISION IS AN INHERENT RISK OF SKIING, DEFENDANT WICKER HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT AS A MATTER OF LAW HIS ACT OF INTENTIONALLY ATTEMPTING TO CUT OFF PLAINTIFF DID NOT INCREASE THE RISK OF COLLISION WITH PLAINTIFF OVER AND ABOVE THOSE RISKS OF COLLISION INHERENT IN THE SPORT OF SKIING. CHEONG V. ANTABLIN, 16 CAL.4TH 1063 (1997). SEE DEPOSITION OF MARCH AT PP. 12, 26; WICKER DEPOSITION AT 13; 17 (INTENT TO CUT OFF). THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT, THEREFORE, AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT WICKER'S CONDUCT WAS INTENTIONAL OR SO RECKLESS AS TO DEFEAT THE ASSUMPTION OF RISK DEFENSE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE
SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MARCH, DANIEL SERVED OCT-10-2011, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT WICKER, JOHN

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAURA RICARDA OF THE BRADY LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 459-7300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JEFFREY V. TA OF THE BLEDSOE LLP, TEL. NO. 981-5411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOHN WICKER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. Although collision is an inherent risk of skiing, Defendant Wicker has failed to demonstrate that as a matter of law his act of intentionally attempting to cut off Plaintiff did not increase the risk of collision with Plaintiff over and above those risks of collision inherent in the sport of skiing. Cheong v. Antablin, 16 Cal.4th 1063 (1997). See Deposition of March at pp. 12, 26; Wicker deposition at 13; 17 (intent to cut off). There is a triable issue of fact, therefore, as to whether Defendant Wicker's conduct was intentional or so reckless as to defeat the assumption of risk defense. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/CK
